observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Budget Officer be directed to comply with the
provisions of the COA 2016-004 pertaining to the timely submission of appropriate
registries and records to the City Accounting Office to facilitate preparation of the SCBAA.
Also, we recommend both the Accounting and Budget Offices to reconcile their records
regularly (monthly, if possible) so that any discrepancies with the records, if cannot be
avoided, detected early and the necessary and timely review and examination can be made
immediately.

We recommended that the LCE directs the City Accountant to faithfully
observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the FS; to revisit the provisions
of the IPSAS and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation
of accounts in the FS; and refer to COA Circular 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016
and its annexes for the preparation and disclosures in FS of Local Government Units.

We recommended that City Accountant apply the perpetual inventory method
and the accounting standards of recording purchases of drugs, medicines, and relief
goods (welfare goods) as Inventories, recognize the corresponding expenses for actual
issuances to the units, and maintain SLC. On the other hand, the General Services
Officer maintain Stock Cards and regularly reconcile with the ledger cards of the
accounting unit.

We recommended that the non-existing or missing PPE items without available
record of accountability be identified and be derecognized from the books of accounts in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts
of government agencies to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence,
condition and accountability.

We recommended that the General Services Office prepares the RLRN and
RPCRNS with the Inventory Committee's help. Furthermore, we recommend that the
City Accountant maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and their components,
reconcile it with the result of the physical count at year-end, correctly depreciate the
accounts, and disclose it in the NFS as prescribed.

We recommended that the management inform the COA office for the deliveries of
procured equipment, goods, materials and equipment for proper inspection of the items
within 24 hours as provided under COA Circular No. 2009-002.

We recommended that a copy of contract or PO, irrespective of amount, and
together with each and every supporting documents, be submitted to the auditor within
5 days upon issuance thereof for the auditorial, legal and technical review.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
(a) the appropriate collecting officer of the LGU, not the BAC, to receive
the collection arising from sale of bidding docs, via an Official Receipt and
properly transmit the same to the City Treasurer’s Office.
(b) the City Treasurer to record the collections in the RCD and deposit such
collections intact in the bank.
(c) the City Accountant to make the necessary Journal Entry to record the
said collection and post the same to the Ledger.

We recommended that the LCE direct the City Accountant and the City
Treasurer to submit all the required monthly financial reports necessary in the audit on
or before the due date.

We recommended that the management discontinue accepting collection
receipts in place of the sales invoice or official receipts as proof of payment. On the
other hand, we recommend that the City Treasurer demand from the supplier for the
sales invoices or official receipts to obtain principal evidence on the payment of goods.

We recommended that the LCE direct the City Treasurer through the City
Accountant to submit to the auditor the 97 cancelled checks for validation and
henceforth, ensure that all cancelled checks are attached to the RCI and reported
chronologically with the other checks issued with the word “Cancelled” clearly
indicated therein.

We recommended that the City Treasurer immediately stamp PAID all DVs and
its relating supporting documents after the payment had been made to prevent possible
reuse of the documents.

We recommended that the LCE:
a. Require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-repair inspection and post
repair inspection to LGU properties that are subjected to repair and furnish the
reports to the COA Auditors within five (5) days from the date of each of the
inspection activity;
b. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain the records regarding the history
of repairs of each of the properties of the LGU; and
c. Direct the City Accountant not to process claims pertaining to repairs and
maintenance of motor vehicles and heavy equipment with lacking supporting
documents.

We recommended that the LCE direct the City Accountant to revisit the provisions
of the IPSAS 42 and to properly disclose the needed disclosures in the FS.

We recommended that the LGU, particularly, the CSWD comply with the pertinent
requirements and regulations concerning the ESP and SAP and submit the lacking
documents to the audit team.

We recommended that the City comply with the requirements prescribed under
LBC 125 and submit the monthly report using the prescribed format (Annex C) to the
auditor's office.
Moreover, (a) direct the City Budget Officer in coordination with the City
Accountant to prepare the required reports on a monthly basis starting from the receipt of
the BGCM until the same will be fully disbursed and furnish copies to the Public
Information Officer and Information and Communication Technology Officer; and (b)
instruct the latter two officers to post the reports in at least three conspicuous places and in
the official website under the transparency seal, respectively and immediately inform the
LCE of such posting so that the latter can send a written notice to the appropriate
government agencies that said reports have been posted in the official website of the LGU.

We recommended that management, specifically the BAC, observe the GPPB
Circular No. 01-2020, GPPB Advisory No. 05-2020, GPPB NPM No. 004-2020 and RIRR
of RA No. 9184 in the emergency procurement under the Bayanihan Act; and submit the
lacking documents to the Audit Team.
Moreover, the City Accountant examine the completeness of the supporting
documents for each disbursement to ensure compliance with the basic and mandatory
documentary requirements.

We recommended that the Management:
a) instruct the Accounting Processor that before effecting payments of claims,
disbursement vouchers should be adequately supported with the required
documents; and
b) comply strictly with Section 4 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-
001.

We recommended that management prepare and submit its CY 2019 Agency
Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) within the period prescribed by the law
using the prescribed form under COA Memorandum No. 2014  002 dated March 18, 2014.
It is further recommended that a focal person be designated by the LCE who will
consolidate the report before submission to the audit team.

The Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to:
a. Comply with the disclosure requirements of the
pertinent PPSAS provisions to have fair presentation of the
account balances in the FS;
b. Strictly observe the accounting, reporting and full
disclosure requirements of DRRMF in the NFS as prescribed
under Annex D of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12,
2012.
c. Provide disclosures as to the Financial Expenses of the
Agency.
d. Account for the cause of the abnormal balance in inventory
and thereafter, prepare adjusting entries

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the City
Accountant require all the depository banks to strictly
comply with the submission of the mandated BS and supporting
documents for all depository accounts maintained by the City
Government within five days after the end of each month. In turn,
within ten days from the receipt of such BS, the same should be
reconciled with the General Ledgers which shall be the basis
for preparation of BRS; and henceforth, the BRS must be
regularly submitted to the Auditor within ten days after the end of
each month together with the paid checks and debit/credit memos.
Likewise, prepare the necessary JEVs to record the reconciling
items affecting the accounts.

We reiterated our prior year recommendation that the
Management direct the City Accountant to reconcile with the City
Treasurer the Cash Accounts records on a quarterly basis to
reflect an accurate and reliable balance at any particular period in
compliance with Paragraph C, Section 181, Volume I of GAAM.

The concerned SDOs properly maintain a cashbook as their cash
records for the proper recording and accounting of their cash
transactions to facilitate determination of their accountabilities and
ensure accurate cash balances, in accordance with Section 28,
Chapter I, Volume II of the MNGAS for LGUs.

We reiterated the following recommendations:
1. Make sure that a cash advance shall be reported on and liquidated as soon as
the purpose for which it was given has been served, or within the periods
required for its settlement;
2. Closely monitor the submission of the liquidation reports by sending:
a. Written reminders (demand letters) to the officers and employees
concerned enjoining them to liquidate their cash advances within the
prescribed periods, or as soon as the purpose for which these were given
have been served, and demand letters in case of failure to comply with
the written reminders.
b. Send tracers to follow-up demand letters sent.
3. Apply requests for write-off of dormant account to the ATL and SA/ RD/ AC,
whichever is applicable, on a quarterly basis. This shall help in reducing the
balances of unliquidated cash advances presented in the books.
4. Exert all possible efforts to reconcile the balances presented in the books and
schedule of cash advances to produce a more accurate presentation of balances.
We also recommend that the management should also consider instituting
sanctions against the officials or employees concerned such as withholding or
suspending payment of their salaries, or other appropriate legal sanction provided
for under the regulation.

The Local Chief Executive to:
a. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to conduct a complete
physical count of PPE, annually, as required and to submit the Report on the
Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE) and Inventory and
Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for the listing of all
Unserviceable Properties to the auditor concerned once completed.
The CGSO shall furnish the CAO the electronic and hard copies of the RPCPPE
for their reconciliation with the accounting records; and
The CAO shall determine any discrepancies between the RPCPPE and
accounting records and prepare the necessary Subsidiary Ledger to record all
unaccounted PPE items. Upon determination of all the City’s unserviceable
assets, the Accountant is tasked to reclassify it to Other Assets account or
dropped it from the books once disposed, whichever is applicable.
b. Intensify efforts to maintain and prepare PPELC with the required format as
shown in Annex 9 on the MNGAS.
c. The City Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for the
depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer should
ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated Property Card
for each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the
preparation of the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of
recording in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation expenses
pertaining to the current year which should be charged against its appropriate
depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in order to present fairly
the PPE accounts with its corresponding Accumulated Depreciation Expense
Accounts in the FS.
d. Moreover, require the heads of the CAO and CGSO to ensure the conduct of
the required annual and semestral physical count of PPE and inventories,
respectively, and timely reconciliation with the Accounting and Supply records
against the related PPELC and Property Cards, and SLC and Stock Cards to
facilitate timely verification and adjustment of any discrepancies noted.
e. Require the CAO to adhere to the prescribed accounting rules relative to the
asset recognition under the Construction in Progress account and to provide
necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the misclassified PPE accounts (CIP
and specific PPE accounts).

(1) Require the coordination between the City Accountant and the GSO
towards the implementation of the perpetual inventory method in the recording of
supplies and materials to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in
the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials;
(2) Determine causes of non-recording of drugs and medicines in the inventory and its
related expense account;
(3) Make proper adjustments in the inventory accounts most especially if the items do
no longer exist. Inventories already issued must be expensed already;
(4) Instruct the GSO and the City Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and
SLC and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate
corrections.
(5) Establish relevant inventory account cited above.

Mark all motor vehicles of the City Government with “For
Official Use only” and to exert an effort that existing motor vehicles
which are not yet government plated be registered accordingly under
which place the name of the City Government.

Provide for Allowance for Impairment Accounts in the books in order to present
fairly the net realizable value of the Receivable Accounts in the financial
statements.

1. Direct the City Treasurer to furnish at the beginning of the year a certified list
of taxpayers with amount due and collectible for the year to the City Accountant
based on the Real Property Tax Register/ Taxpayer’s Index Card and make an
effort to collect all taxes due to the LGU.
2. Instruct the City Accountant to use this as a basis in recording Real Property
Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable; adjust the Real Property
Tax and Special Education Tax to present a more reliable and accurate balance.

Direct the City Accountant to timely submit the Statement of Receipts and
Disbursements and related supporting documents to the source agencies to avoid
non-reconciliation of balances as well as to avoid the risk that they may not be
able to be given additional funding from the National Government.

Furnish the Audit Team with copies of MOA/Trust Agreement on all funds
transferred by the NGAs to evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the
specified purpose.

Furnish the Auditor copy of the POs and contract documents not later than
five (5) working days from perfection and issuance of contracts together with
duly accomplished supporting documents for technical review and evaluation.

The management has already submitted the list of on-going projects to the
auditor: CY2018 projects on May 3, 2019 and CY2019 projects on January 3,
2020.

• Formulate a policy on the granting of FA systematically to determine the
extent of aid to be given to individuals in need.
Beneficiaries should be properly screened giving priority to the less
privileged constituents.
• Coordinate with the CSWO to assess request for medical and
hospitalization assistance by way of Social Case Study Reports so that only
the deserving indigents can be assisted and served.
• Instruct the CSWO to require the applicants for AICS a complete submission
of supporting documents for the assistance being requested as a basis of
assessment and recommendation to make sure that only the deserving ones
were provided with the necessary assistance to help them cope during crisis
situations.

a) Appropriate one percent (1%) of its Annual Budget for the Senior
Citizen and PWD and ensure optimal utilization of the fund to fully uphold the
goal of addressing the concerns of senior citizens and persons with disability;
and,
b) Make sure that charges intended for Senior Citizens and Person with
Disabilities are made in the appropriate fund.

Utilize solely the LDRRMF to projects and activities under the four (4)
thematic areas, namely, disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster
preparedness, disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery, and
refrain from incurring expenses not related to disaster risk management
activities to DRRM Fund.

a) Require the GAD Focal Person to prepare on time the annual GPB for
review and approval by DILG and submit the approved copy and the
corresponding AR to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed period; and
b) Approve charges and disbursements on GAD Fund only if supported with
DILG reviewed and approved GPB to ascertain validity and propriety of
disbursements.

The personnel-in-charge of the program to: (1) Submit the Project Proposal
Program of both Feeding and Livelihood Program of the City; and (2) Submit
the Monitoring report pertaining to the Feeding Program of the Agency.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension (NS) where necessary.

Ensure that the utilization of the 20% DF be limited to PPAs that partake the
nature of investment or capital expenditures, and refrain from appropriating or
including in the appropriation of development projects the personal services,
not allowable to be charged to the 20% DF.

1. Ensure that no additional cash advance shall be granted unless the previous cash advance is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made; 2. Make sure that a cash advance shall be reported on and liquidated as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served, or within the periods required for its settlement; 3. Closely monitor the submission of the liquidation reports by sending: a. Written reminders (demand letters) to the officers and employees concerned enjoining them to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed periods, or as soon as the purpose for which these were given have been served, and demand letters in case of failure to comply with the written reminders. b. Send tracers to follow-up demand letters sent. 4. Apply requests for write-off of dormant account to the ATL and SA/ RD/ AC, whichever is applicable, on a quarterly basis. This shall help in reducing the balances of unliquidated cash advances presented in the books. 5. Exert all possible efforts to reconcile the balances presented in the books and schedule of cash advances to produce a more accurate presentation of balances. Also consider instituting sanctions against the officials or employees concerned such as withholding or suspending payment of their salaries, or other appropriate legal sanction provided for under the regulation.

a. Upon determination of all the City’s unserviceable assets, the Accountant is tasked to reclassify it to Other Assets account or dropped it from the books once disposed, whichever is applicable. b. The City Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for the depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer should ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated Property Card for each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the preparation of the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of recording in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation expenses pertaining to the current year which should be charged against its appropriate depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in order to present fairly the PPE accounts with its corresponding Accumulated Depreciation Expense Accounts in the FS. Moreover, require the heads of the CAO and CGSO to ensure the conduct of the required annual and semestral physical count of PPE and inventories, respectively, and timely reconciliation with the Accounting and Supply records against the related PPELC and Property Cards, and SLC and Stock Cards to facilitate timely verification and adjustment of any discrepancies noted.

pertaining to the current year which should be charged against its appropriate
depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in order to present fairly
the PPE accounts with its corresponding Accumulated Depreciation Expense
Accounts in the Financial Statements.
Moreover, require the heads of the Accounting Office and GSO to ensure
the conduct of the required annual and semestral physical count of PPE and
inventories, respectively, and timely reconciliation with the Accounting and
Supply records against the related PPELC and Property Cards, and SLC and
Stock Cards to facilitate timely verification and adjustment of any discrepancies
noted.

We recommended that management facilitate the preparation of documentary
requirements to secure Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) of purchased lots to
support claims of ownership. Prepare plans to initiate activities on how to pursue
complete titling process of the lots promptly in order to present the correct amount of
its Land account recorded in the books of accounts. For this purpose, management
should have the land registered in the name of the City, with the Registry of Deeds.
The Land recorded in the books which were not supported with TCT and
other legal documents of ownership should be properly disclosed in the Notes to
Financial Statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor to:
1. Direct the Head of the City Health Office to forward the necessary supporting
documents of the donated properties to the GSO and Accounting Office; and
2. The GSO and the accounting office shall record all donated assets in the property
and accounting records respectively based on the deeds of donations being
furnished by the City Health Office. These offices should coordinate to ensure
compliance with regulations on donated assets. Moreover, an appraisal of donated
properties must be made by the City Appraisal Committee as a basis for
recording.

We reiterated our prior year recommendation that the Management direct
the City Accountant to exert all possible efforts to reconcile the balances
presented in the books and schedule of Investment Property to produce a more
accurate presentation of balances.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Direct the Isabela City Inventory and Disposal Committee to facilitate the
completion of 2018 Inventory Count and to submit the RPCI by inventory
account name and distribute the same as prescribed; to reconcile said report
with the records of the City Accounting Office; and to submit such report to
the auditor concerned once complete.
b. Instruct the GSO and the City Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards
and Supply Ledger Cards and regularly reconcile records to detect
discrepancies and effect immediate corrections; and for the GSO to prepare
and submit the monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) to
the City Accountant.
Moreover, require the heads of the Accounting Office and GSO to ensure
the conduct of the required annual and semestral physical count of PPE and
inventories, respectively, and timely reconciliation with the Accounting and
Supply records against the related PPELC and Property Cards, and SLC and
Stock Cards to facilitate timely verification and adjustment of any discrepancies
noted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall direct the City
Accountant to submit all the required financial records and statements not later than
the fourteenth day of February each year covering the year’s transactions so as not to
cause delay in the preparation of the annual report on the financial condition and
results of operation of the municipality which is vital in decision making of the City.

We reiterated our prior year recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:
(1) Require the City Accountant to submit the Report of Checks Issued and
Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office
pursuant to Section 55 on the MNGAS and to facilitate submission to the
Auditor within the prescribed period;
(2) Instruct the City Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily
basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the City
Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial
statements; and
(3) Require the City Accountant to submit financial reports, disbursement
vouchers and supporting documents within the prescribed period.

We reiterate our prior year recommendation to direct the personnel assigned
to prepare the quarterly PPA reports to include non-infrastructure projects in
compliance with COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Isabela City
Inventory and Disposal Committee to conduct a complete physical count inventory,
annually, as required; Likewise, the RPCPPE and PIF should be furnished to the
Audit Team for review and verification. This will serve as guide in identifying
insurable properties of the City.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct:
1. The City Treasurer to intensify efforts in collecting Real Property Taxes, a
primary source of SEF funds;
2. The City School Board to provide detailed computation to arrive at the total
SEF budget, to particularize the items of expenses charged against SEF, and
providing schedule of expenses and purpose of the said expenditure;
3. The City Accountant to provide justification for the unreconciled beginning
SEF balance and to submit the SEF ledgers.

We reiterate our recommendation from previous year for the City Government
to utilize the SEF Budget other than Sports Development for the improvement and
development of public schools and its students in the City of Isabela. Other items for
which the fund may be used are enumerated in Section 272, RA 7160 which could
serve as LGU’s guidance.

We reiterate our previous’ year recommendation to:
(1) Adhere to the rules and regulations set forth in R.A. No. 10121 in setting
aside of at least 5% DRRM fund from its estimated revenue from regular
sources;
(2) Require the LDRRM Officer to submit the Monthly Report on Sources
and Utilization of DRRMF within 15th day after the end of each month
and not on a quarterly basis.
Moreover, we recommend that the City Accountant to strictly observe the
accounting, reporting and full disclosure requirements of DRRMF in the Notes to
Financial Statements as prescribed under Annex D of COA Circular 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Isabela City Solid
Waste Management Board (SWMB) to submit its 2018 Solid Waste Accomplishment
Report to the City Auditor. Moreover, require the SWMB to ensure the annual
submission of its Solid Waste Accomplishment Report.

We reiterated our prior year recommendation to the Management:
a. Comply with the required 1% allocation of the IRA of barangays,
municipalities and cities for the strengthening and implementation of the
programs of the LCPC.
Likewise, avoid allocating Children Welfare Related Programs in the
GAD unless it addresses specific gender related issues among children.
b. Maximize the use of funds and perform other activities and programs
related to Local Protection of Children per Section II of DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We reiterate our prior year recommendation that the LGU comply with the
mandatory 5% budgetary requirement.
The Local Chief Executive should also direct the GFPS:
1. To endorse the City’s GAD Plan and Budget to the PPDO to ensure the
alignment of the municipal/city GAD PPAs with the priorities of the
province and then from the PPDO to the DILG Provincial Office for its
review and endorsement.
2. To provide justification as to how the activities conducted by the LGU
pertaining to children in need of special protection, out of school youth,
malnutrition among pre-school children, rights and welfare of Persons
with Disability (PWDs) and Senior Citizens relates to the gender-
responsive, rights-based and culture-sensitive projects, programs and
activities.
3. Provide separate appropriation PPAs attributed to the Senior Citizens and
PWDs.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended:
That management justify the above noted lapses. Henceforth ensure that the
utilization of the 20% DF be limited to PPAs that partake the nature of investment or
capital expenditures in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2017-1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the City Engineer and
other officials concerned for the implementation of projects to (i) submit an explanation
for the non-implementation of the PPAs in accordance with the plans and targets for the
year; (ii) regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary
interventions or rectification may be introduced at the earliest time; and (iii) abide by the
target project timelines for the future projects to maximize the developmental benefits
and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the management to direct the persons responsible to settle
the audit suspension through compliance with the requirements indicated in the Notice of
Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for the audit disallowance and charge must settle
the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009,
otherwise known as the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts.

We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  and  the  Municipal
   Accountant strictly require officials and employees who have unliquidated
   cash advances to settle the amount advanced within the prescribed periods
   pursuant to Section 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002.  Otherwise, recommend
   for the withholding of their salaries pursuant  to  second  paragraph  of
   Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 until such time  that  the  cash
   advances are fully settled.

We  recommended  that  the  Inventory  Committee  completes  its
   physical inventory  taking,  render  a  report  thereon  and  update  and
   reconcile the related subsidiary records to have assurance that  the  PPE
   accounts totaling P223,180,244.14 are presented fairly in  the  Statement
   of Financial Position as of December 31, 2018.


            We further recommended that for all existing properties, as well

   inventory taking


              Henceforth, periodic inventory taking  and  reconciliation  of

We recommended that Municipal Mayor require  the  seller  (Jose
   Mari Y. Villa) to immediately process for the issuance of a TCT  in  the
   name of the Municipality for the lot purchased so as to assure ownership
   and prevent exposure of government property to possible loss.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that funds appropriated for the 20% development fund be fully utilized in the implementation of projects to serve the purpose/s provided for so that the service and benefits expected from these projects are delivered to the constituents without necessary delay. We also recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator in accordance with Section 476 of RA 7160 shall formulate integrated economic, social, physical, and the other development plans and policies for consideration of the local government development council and monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different development programs, projects and activities in the local government unit concerned in accordance with the approved development plan. Representation shall be made from time to time with the Local Chief Executive of the 20% development Fund.

We further recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer thoroughly Examine the projects in the continuing appropriations at year-end, especially those that remained unobligated. Representation to the Municipal Mayor shall be made that the appropriations no longer needed will be recommended for immediate reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for appropriation to various economic, social development and environmental programs. We further recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator in accordance with Section 476 of RA 7160 shall formulate integrated economic, social, physical, and the other development plans and policies for consideration of the local government development council and monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different development programs, projects and activities in the local government unit concerned in accordance with the approved development plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the annual inventory taking and submission of the Report of Physical Count of PPE for verification and review. Management should ensure that they can achieve the LGU’s financial reporting objective of issuing timely and reliable financial reports/statements.

Further, we recommend that City Accountant see to it that depreciation are provided annually in accordance with regulations and to ensure the proper valuation and presentation of PPE in the financial statements. 

In addition the Accounting Unit and GSO or concerned Property Officer should maintain updated Property Card for each PPE Account and for each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the preparation  of the Lapsing Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of recording in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation expenses. These are necessary in order to present fairly the PPE accounts with the corresponding Accumulated Depreciation Expenses in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

   a. Direct the Chief Accountant to update the Subsidiary  Ledgers  of  all
      the accounts of the City to  present  reliable  data  on  the  nature,
      status and balances of the accounts.

   b.  Refer to the available records and files of financial transactions of
      the agency, and record the entries to the SL in  order  to  arrive  at
      reliable balances which correspond to the amount stated in the General
      Ledgers of the major accounts.

a. Management should treat the matter of physical inventory and appraisal
      of the PPE, a priority concern.  A schedule  of  PPE  and  a  Physical
      Inventory Report showing at least the acquisition costs of the  assets
      comprising PPE should be accomplished and submitted  for  verification
      and review.  They should ensure that they can  achieve  its  financial
      reporting  objective  of  issuing  timely   and   reliable   financial
      statements.  A  separate  inventory  report  shall  be  prepared   for
      unserviceable PPE which do not contribute to the economic activity  of
      the City. This unserviceable  report  shall  be  treated  under  Other
      Assets Account.

   b. The City Accountant and other Officers concerned  should  provide  for
      the depreciation on the  depreciable  assets.    The  Property  Supply
      Officer should ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and  maintains
      updated  Property  Card  for  each  PPE  Account  to  readily  provide
      pertinent data, thus facilitate the preparation  of  the  Schedule  of
      Depreciation which shall be the basis of  recording in  the  books  of
      accounts the  appropriate  depreciation  expenses  pertaining  to  the
      current  year  which  should  be  charged  against   its   appropriate
      depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in order to present
      fairly  the  PPE   Accounts   with   its   corresponding   Accumulated
      Depreciation Expenses Account in the financial statements.

We recommended that management require the Accountant to –

   a. Ensure that no additional cash advance shall  be  granted  unless  the
      previous cash advance is first settled or a proper accounting  thereof
      is mad;

   b. See to it that a cash advance shall be reported on and  liquidated  as
      soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served, or  within
      the periods requires for its settlement;

   c. Closely monitor the submission of the liquidation reports by sending:

        ▪  Written  reminders  to  the  officers  and  employees  concerned
          enjoining them  to  liquidate  their  cash  advances  within  the
          prescribed periods, or as soon as the  purpose  for  which  these
          were given have been  served,  and  demand  letters  in  case  of
          failure to comply with the written reminders.

        ▪ Send tracers to follow-up demand letters sent.

a. Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the City Treasurer  and
      the City Accountant should both do the reconciliation of  the  cash
      in vault account.  They  should  reconcile  their  books  at  least
      quarterly as directed under Section 181 (c) of COA Circular No. 91-
      368 dated December 19, 1991. Any error,  omission  or  misstatement
      appearing  in  the  books  of   accounts   shall   be   the   joint
      responsibility of the City Accountant and City Treasurer.

     b. We further  recommended  that  the  City  treasurer  and  the  City
        Accountant exert their best effort to trace, verify  and  reconcile
        the balances of the Cash in Bank Accounts starting  from  the  last
        date the books had been balanced. Copies of bank debit  and  credit
        memoranda should be secured to support the  entries  that  will  be
        prepared to adjust or correct the accounts. Documentation from  the
        banks should be persistently secured or  serve  as  basis  for  the
        adjustments/corrections and recording of changes made by the  banks
        on the City’s Accounts.

     c. The City Accountant is likewise required to conscientiously  obtain
        from the Authorized Government Depository Banks (AGDB) the  monthly
        Bank Statements together with the paid  checks  all  bank  accounts
        maintained by the City. These important documents are necessary for
        the preparation of the required BRSs and its ultimate submission to
        the Auditor in compliance with the provisions of Section 3.2of  COA
        Circular No. 96-00 dated October 2, 1996.
